posts.name: Simon Ojstersek
posts.cooked: Is there possibility to downgrade Universal Tuya app?
A „downgrade“ is only possible in case you happen to have downloaded the appropriate source code and install it as your development version

posts.name: Dylan Rajasekaram
posts.cooked:
Just pushed v5.11.154 to the test channel.

v5.11.152: Fixed rain sensor pairing by removing strict endpoints., Removed emoji characters from all driver.js files to prevent SyntaxError crashes on Homey Pro.

Covers a huge range of Tuya devices at this point.

posts.name: Lasse K
posts.cooked: Ver.154. Contact sensor installs as unknown Zigbee, BUT has full correct function.

posts.name: Robert Klep
posts.cooked:
Lasse_K:

Contact sensor installs as unknown Zigbee, BUT has full correct function.

Probably the recently improved generic Zigbee device support that’s handling it.

image1372×208 10.5 KB

posts.name: Dylan Rajasekaram
posts.cooked:
Hey @Lasse_K, I totally understand your frustration with the contact sensor showing up as unknown. It’s likely related to the recent improvements in Zigbee support. If everything is working fine, that’s a relief! But if you want to clear that “unknown” label, re-pairing could definitely help.

@robertklep, you’re spot on about the functionality still being there despite the unknown status. It can be a bit quirky with some devices, but as long as it’s doing its job, that’s what counts! If you start seeing any missing readings or anything unusual, feel free to share the app logs here, and I’ll take a look at the data points for you.

I’m here if you need anything else!

posts.name: Sven
posts.cooked: But AFAIK Tuya uses its own clusters (not the generic clusters included in ZigBee), right? That wouldn’t work in that case as I believe Homey only added support for the generic clusters.

posts.name: Robert Klep
posts.cooked: Many of my Tuya sensors use standard clusters.

posts.name: Lasse K
posts.cooked: It explains why my Watersensor also installs as unknown, but has full function. Thanks a lot
